questions.topicPhrase: life today compared with life 100 years ago
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about life in the past. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: 'Life is better today than it was 100 years ago.' Do you agree?
questions.claim: life today is far better than it was 100 years ago
questions.counterpoint: some traditions and simpler ways of life have been lost

points.clause: healthcare has advanced enormously
points.phrase: advances in healthcare

points.clause: entertainment options have expanded dramatically
points.phrase: the wide range of entertainment available today

points.clause: technology has transformed how people communicate
points.phrase: improvements in communication technology

questions.topicPhrase: earning a high salary compared with enjoying one's job
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about work. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: Is it better to earn a lot of money or to enjoy your job?
writingPoints: how much time is spent at work
the type of work which is done
questions.claim: many people believe a high salary matters more than job satisfaction
questions.counterpoint: others feel that happiness at work is what truly matters

questions.topicPhrase: protecting animals in danger of extinction
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about animals and the environment. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: 'We should do everything we can to save animals which are in danger of disappearing from our planet.' Do you agree?
writingPoints: the kind of animals which are in danger
the reasons for protecting these animals
questions.claim: we should do everything possible to save endangered animals
questions.counterpoint: conservation efforts can be expensive and difficult to enforce

points.clause: many iconic species such as tigers and rhinos are close to extinction
points.phrase: the plight of iconic species like tigers and rhinos

points.clause: every species plays a vital role in maintaining ecological balance
points.phrase: the vital ecological role each species plays

points.clause: losing key species can disrupt entire food chains
points.phrase: the risk of disrupting entire food chains

questions.topicPhrase: the relative importance of friends and family
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about relationships. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: Which is more important – friends or family?
writingPoints: who you can enjoy yourself with
who will help you when you have problems
questions.claim: friends and family both play essential but different roles in our lives
questions.counterpoint: the importance of each can change depending on the situation

questions.topicPhrase: pollution and damage to the environment
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about the environment.
questions.mainQuestion: Every country in the world has problems with pollution and damage to the environment. Do you think these problems can be solved?
questions.claim: pollution is damaging the environment on a global scale
questions.counterpoint: solving it will require cooperation from governments, businesses and individuals alike

points.clause: vehicle emissions are polluting the air in most major cities
points.phrase: emissions from transport

points.clause: industrial waste and plastic are contaminating rivers and seas
points.phrase: the contamination of rivers and seas

points.clause: switching to renewable energy could cut harmful emissions significantly
points.phrase: a shift towards renewable energy

questions.topicPhrase: the fashion industry's effect on people's lives
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about the fashion industry.
questions.mainQuestion: Some people say the fashion industry has a bad effect on people's lives. Do you agree?
questions.claim: the fashion industry has a largely negative effect on people's lives
questions.counterpoint: it also creates jobs and allows for creative self-expression

points.clause: advertising promotes unrealistic standards of appearance
points.phrase: the pressure to look a certain way

points.clause: fashionable clothes are often priced far beyond what ordinary families can afford
points.phrase: the high price of fashionable clothes

points.clause: fast fashion generates enormous amounts of waste
points.phrase: the environmental damage caused by fast fashion

questions.topicPhrase: learning a foreign language
questions.context: In your English class, you have been talking about learning languages. Now your English teacher has asked you to write an essay for homework.
questions.mainQuestion: There are more reasons to learn a foreign language than just to pass a test. Do you agree?
questions.claim: there are far more reasons to learn a language than simply passing a test
questions.counterpoint: exams do at least provide a clear, structured goal

points.clause: learning new words and expressions can be genuinely enjoyable
points.phrase: the sheer enjoyment of discovering a new language

points.clause: mastering a language is a rewarding personal challenge
points.phrase: the personal challenge of mastering a language

points.clause: speaking another language can open up valuable career opportunities
points.phrase: the career opportunities a second language brings

questions.topicPhrase: teaching young people the history of their own country
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about learning history at school. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: Everyone should be taught the history of their own country. Do you agree?
writingPoints: what people can learn from the past
it's more important to think about the future
questions.claim: everyone should be taught the history of their own country
questions.counterpoint: some argue that time would be better spent focusing on the future

points.clause: understanding past mistakes helps us avoid repeating them
points.phrase: the lessons history teaches us

points.clause: focusing only on the future ignores the context the past provides
points.phrase: the context history provides for the present

points.clause: knowing our shared past strengthens a sense of national identity
points.phrase: a stronger sense of national identity

questions.topicPhrase: technology's effect on how sociable people are
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about the impact of technology on communication.
questions.mainQuestion: Some people believe that technology is making us less sociable. Do you agree?
questions.claim: technology is changing how sociable people are
questions.counterpoint: it also helps people stay in touch in ways that were never possible before

questions.topicPhrase: shopping online rather than in traditional stores
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about changes in how people shop.
questions.mainQuestion: Online shopping is better than shopping in traditional stores. Do you agree?
questions.claim: online shopping has become more popular than traditional shopping
questions.counterpoint: physical stores still offer things the internet cannot replace

questions.topicPhrase: the amount of money paid to famous sports people
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about money for sports people. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: 'Famous sports people are paid too much money.' Do you agree?
questions.claim: famous sports people are paid far too much money
questions.counterpoint: many argue their short careers and rare talent justify the high pay

points.clause: top athletes entertain millions of fans around the world
points.phrase: the entertainment they provide to millions

points.clause: professional athletes train for years to reach the top of their sport
points.phrase: the years of hard training behind their success

points.clause: many athletes earn more from sponsorship deals than from playing itself
points.phrase: the vast sums earned through sponsorship deals

questions.topicPhrase: being self-employed rather than working for someone else
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about self-employment. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: Is it better to be self-employed or to work for somebody else?
questions.claim: self-employment offers a very different lifestyle to working for someone else
questions.counterpoint: both options suit different types of people

questions.topicPhrase: the effect of advertising on society
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about advertising. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: 'Advertising is a very good thing for society.' Do you agree?
questions.claim: advertising does more harm than good for society
questions.counterpoint: it does help keep people informed about new products and prices

points.clause: advertising does help people learn about new products and offers
points.phrase: the useful information advertising provides

points.clause: advertising encourages companies to compete on price and quality
points.phrase: the competition it encourages between companies

points.clause: advertisers often manipulate people into buying things they do not need
points.phrase: the way advertising manipulates consumers

questions.topicPhrase: helping other people on a regular basis
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about helping other people. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: 'Everyone should do something regularly in their life to help other people.' Do you agree?
writingPoints: the importance of friends and family
questions.claim: everyone should do something regularly to help other people
questions.counterpoint: busy lives can make this difficult in practice

points.clause: helping friends and family strengthens our closest relationships
points.phrase: the way it strengthens relationships with friends and family

points.clause: volunteering helps build stronger, more supportive communities
points.phrase: the contribution it makes to the wider community

points.clause: helping others builds empathy and valuable new skills
points.phrase: the personal growth that comes from helping others

questions.topicPhrase: making sport compulsory at school
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about sport. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: 'All students should have to do sport at school.' Do you agree?
questions.claim: all students should be required to do sport at school
questions.counterpoint: some students genuinely dislike sport and feel forced into it

points.clause: team sports teach students how to cooperate and communicate
points.phrase: the teamwork skills sport teaches

points.clause: some students strongly dislike sport and find it stressful
points.phrase: the fact that some students genuinely dislike sport

points.clause: regular exercise brings enormous benefits for physical and mental health
points.phrase: the health benefits of regular exercise

questions.topicPhrase: advertising aimed at children
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about advertising. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: 'Advertisements which are aimed at children should not be allowed.' What do you think?
writingPoints: They can be entertaining and fun.
questions.claim: advertisements aimed at children should not be allowed
questions.counterpoint: some of these adverts are genuinely creative and entertaining

points.clause: some adverts aimed at children are creative and entertaining
points.phrase: their entertainment value

points.clause: children often pressure their parents into buying things they do not need
points.phrase: the unnecessary spending they encourage

points.clause: children lack the skills to recognise manipulative advertising
points.phrase: the psychological impact on young children

questions.topicPhrase: belonging to a large family rather than a small one
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about family life. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: Is it better to be part of a large family or a small family?
questions.claim: family size can shape a child's upbringing in very different ways
questions.counterpoint: the right size really depends on each family's circumstances

questions.topicPhrase: the effect technology will have on our lives in the future
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about technology and how it's changing. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: Do you think technology will improve our lives in the future?
questions.claim: technology will continue to improve our lives in the years ahead
questions.counterpoint: some fear that rapid change also brings serious risks

points.clause: technology will make communicating across the world even easier
points.phrase: improvements in global communication

points.clause: automation will take over dangerous and repetitive jobs
points.phrase: changes in employment brought by automation

points.clause: artificial intelligence could revolutionise how diseases are diagnosed
points.phrase: advances in AI-driven healthcare

questions.topicPhrase: taking a gap year before university
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about what students do after finishing school. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: Is taking a gap year before university a good idea?
questions.claim: taking a gap year before university can be a good idea
questions.counterpoint: it does not suit every student's circumstances

questions.topicPhrase: reducing childhood obesity
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about health and young people. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: What can be done to reduce childhood obesity?
questions.claim: childhood obesity has become a serious health problem
questions.counterpoint: tackling it will require action on several fronts at once

points.clause: many children eat far too much sugary and processed food
points.phrase: unhealthy eating habits

points.clause: children today spend far less time being physically active
points.phrase: a lack of regular exercise

points.clause: schools can teach healthier habits and offer better food choices
points.phrase: better education about nutrition in schools

questions.topicPhrase: social media addiction among young people
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about social media and its effects. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: What are the causes and effects of social media addiction among young people?
questions.claim: social media addiction has become a serious problem among young people
questions.counterpoint: the platforms themselves are partly designed to encourage this behaviour

points.clause: constant notifications train users to check their phones compulsively
points.phrase: the constant stream of notifications

points.clause: comparing themselves to others online damages young people's self-esteem
points.phrase: harmful comparisons with others online

points.clause: heavy use is linked to rising anxiety and depression among teenagers
points.phrase: its damaging effect on mental health

questions.topicPhrase: working from home rather than in an office
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about modern working conditions. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: Do you agree that working from home is better than working in an office?
questions.claim: working from home is better than working in an office for many people
questions.counterpoint: it does not suit every job or every personality

questions.topicPhrase: the growing popularity of electric cars
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about transport and the environment. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: Why are electric cars becoming more popular, and how might this trend affect the future?
questions.claim: electric cars are becoming increasingly popular
questions.counterpoint: challenges around cost and charging infrastructure remain

points.clause: concern about climate change is pushing drivers towards cleaner alternatives
points.phrase: growing concern about climate change

points.clause: government incentives are making electric cars more affordable
points.phrase: generous government incentives

points.clause: improvements in battery technology are making electric cars more practical
points.phrase: rapid improvements in battery technology

questions.topicPhrase: online learning compared with traditional classroom learning
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about education and technology. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: Is online learning better than traditional classroom learning?
questions.claim: online learning is becoming a serious alternative to the traditional classroom
questions.counterpoint: many students still value the structure a classroom provides

questions.topicPhrase: the effect pets have on people's mental health and wellbeing
questions.context: In your English class you have been talking about pets and their role in people's lives. Now, your English teacher has asked you to write an essay.
questions.mainQuestion: Do pets improve people's mental health and wellbeing?
questions.claim: pets genuinely improve people's mental health and wellbeing
questions.counterpoint: keeping a pet also brings real responsibilities and costs

points.clause: pets provide companionship that helps reduce feelings of loneliness
points.phrase: the companionship they provide

points.clause: owning a pet comes with real responsibilities and ongoing costs
points.phrase: the responsibility and cost involved

points.clause: spending time with an animal has been shown to lower stress levels
points.phrase: the stress relief that comes from spending time with animals
